trade.summary: Trade has grown significantly. Indonesia exports palm oil, rubber, coal, and textiles. Turchia exports machinery, vehicles, chemicals, and steel. Turchia has aggressively pursued trade agreements with ASEAN nations. A bilateral FTA has been under negotiation.

diplomatic.summary: Both are large Muslim-majority countries and members of G20. They cooperate through OIC and D-8 (Developing 8 Countries). Both share a non-aligned stance and are active in Global South diplomacy. Turchia and Indonesia coordinate on Palestinian cause. MIKTA membership overlaps partially. President Erdogan has visited Indonesia and both leaders maintain warm ties.

military.summary: Growing defence cooperation. Turchia has offered Bayraktar drones and defence equipment to Indonesia. Defence industry MoUs have been signed. Both countries participate in some joint defence industry fairs.

financial.summary: Limited but growing FDI flows. Turkish construction companies have explored Indonesian infrastructure projects. Indonesian palm oil companies have invested in Turkish refining capacity.

energy.summary: Turchia imports Indonesian coal as an alternative coal supplier. Some Turkish energy companies have explored opportunities in Indonesian renewable energy sector.

scientific.summary: Growing academic exchanges. Turkish Maarif Foundation operates schools in Indonesia. Some scientific cooperation in Islamic studies, archaeology, and engineering through bilateral agreements.
